description: Ion CodeGenerator OsiSpace — Missing ensureOsiSpace() Before Call Sites + Erroneous lastOsiPointOffset_ Advance Lets Consecutive OSI Patches Overlap. CVE-2023-25751 (sec-high, MFSA 2023-09, Firefox 111). Ion's on-stack invalidation (OSI) mechanism lets the GC atomically replace a live jitcode call return address with a bailout stub when the underlying Ion code has been invalidated (e.g. by shape changes or compacting GC). An OSI point is patched by overwriting a `PatchWrite_NearCallSize()` (typically 5 bytes on x86_64) span of instructions immediately before the associated `callJit` / `callWithABI`. To guarantee that span is always present, `js::jit::CodeGeneratorShared::ensureOsiSpace()` in js/src/jit/shared/CodeGenerator-shared.cpp pads the code stream with no-ops until `masm.currentOffset() - lastOsiPointOffset_ >= PatchWrite_NearCallSize()`. The vulnerable version had two interacting defects. First, in js/src/jit/CodeGenerator.cpp, several Ion call-emitting routines — `callVMInternal`, `visitCallNative`, `visitCallDOMNative`, `visitCallGeneric`, `visitCallKnown`, `emitApplyGeneric`, `visitGetDOMProperty`, `visitSetDOMProperty`, `emitIonToWasmCallBase` — emitted `masm.callJit` / `masm.callWithABI` *without* calling `ensureOsiSpace()` first, so the code immediately preceding the call was whatever the previous codegen had emitted (often only 2–3 bytes of instructions). Second, `CodeGeneratorShared::ensureOsiSpace()` itself contained a bogus trailing assignment `lastOsiPointOffset_ = masm.currentOffset();` which advanced the invariant anchor even when no OSI point was actually being emitted — so subsequent checks saw the span as satisfied when it was not. The combined effect is that two OSI points can sit closer together in the Ion code stream than `PatchWrite_NearCallSize()`. When the GC later patches the second OSI point, the 5-byte near-call instruction it writes overlaps the bytes of the first OSI point, corrupting that earlier instruction. Execution resuming through the corrupted instruction lands in middle-of-instruction bytes, producing either a wildpointer crash (the debug assertion in the Ion frame iterator fires: `PatchableBackedge` invariants) or, in release builds, arbitrary-looking control flow that an attacker can use as a JIT miscompile primitive. The fix (commit d307cab06d56b4be62796b083b16feb86a47829c by Iain Ireland, 2023-02-13, r=jandem; Phabricator D169274) adds `ensureOsiSpace();` before each of the nine call-site emitters in CodeGenerator.cpp and removes the stray `lastOsiPointOffset_ = masm.currentOffset();` from CodeGenerator-shared.cpp so the invariant is anchored only by actual OSI-point emission in `markOsiPoint`. The in-tree regression test (`js/src/jit-test/tests/ion/bug1814899.js`, committed separately as 5ba1f3caa6dd8 on 2023-04-25) ships as poc.js verbatim: it sets up a bar/foo mutual-recursion pair inside a `with({})` scope, uses a `for..in` over a string to exercise the `visitCallGeneric`/`visitCallNative` path, and drops `gczeal(14, 1)` inside `bar` to force compacting GC patching of the Ion code while the colliding OSI points are live.
